entities.name: 职业倦怠 (burnout)
entities.how_described: 本集数据显示它是科技圈的一大痛点，中重度倦怠比例从去年的 44.7% 飙升至今年的 54.7%。报告指出，虽然 AI 让大家交付得更快，但人们承担了过多的原型、PR 等任务，导致了更多倦怠。

evidence.quote: Burnout at work is a major problem and AI could add to this already overwhelmed employee problem.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: Burnout is increasing significantly.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: I believe this is the largest survey of its kind, looking at how people in tech feel about their jobs and AI and layoffs and burnout, and the future of their careers.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: We were seeing fairly high levels of burnout, but also fairly good levels of optimism about where things are going.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And we actually followed up on the burnout point and we created the Armor Framework, if you remember.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: So let's actually look at that data of just how this sort of classification connects to just optimism, burnout, things like that.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: Yeah, so we'll dive into some of these things in a little more depth later on, but just to put it all together, what you're looking at here is the AI identity stance, and then a breakdown by your AI identity around career optimism, burnout, layoff worry.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: Burnout, or reports of burnout, go up significantly.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: Let's talk about burnout and optimism.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: So, last year we looked at burnout and we looked at optimism, and burnout was at a worrying level, enough that we published further research on it.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And we introduced the Armor Framework around how to deal with burnout and fight burnout.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: I think I was hoping that optimism would hold steady, and that burnout wouldn't go up, because supposedly with new abilities and even better models, perhaps you might think that we wouldn't have to work as hard, or put as much energy into everything in a way that gets us burnt out.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: When it comes to significant burnout, burnout that's higher than moderate, we've gone up from 44.7% in 2025 to 54.7% in 2026.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: So burnout is surging.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: Pretty worrisome, the rise to a massive jump, over 10% increase in burnout, just in a year.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: I loved your conversation with Jeff from Ramp, and he shared that his worst burnout happened when velocity was lowest.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: So when you put effort into things that don't actually move, it doesn't feel good and that leads to burnout.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And I think that maps for me with certain theories around burnout, where stagnation leads to burnout.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: We're just taking on more stuff, more stuff, more prototypes, more PRDs, more PRs, more campaigns, more agents, more ads, and it's leading to more burnout.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: I don't know if you have this in this deck, but a glimmer of hope that you pointed out is that enjoyment of work is actually still very high, that it's burnout, but also people still at the same levels last year actually, really enjoying their work.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: But before we get there, one of the other really interesting takeaways was around layoffs and how layoffs impact fear of layoffs impact burnout.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: At the same time, just broadly, burnout is increasing significantly.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: My people are having a really bad time, but then broadly, this optimism and burnout shift.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: Obviously connects to that burnout point we made earlier.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: The old type of emotion, the old type of burnout was just completely grim.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: But yes, when we look at founders and when we look at, in this case, you're looking at company size as well, you can see that whether it's optimism, whether it's burnout, layoff worry, or whether people would recommend their job.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: They have the lowest burnout, the lowest layoff worry, the most AI excitement.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And if we look just at burnout specifically, then you can see very clearly here that burnout climbs in very significant ways in between working for a small 1 to 10 person startup and working for a 5,000 or 10,000 person enterprise company.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: I would say one thing to highlight here, if you look at burnout, for example, is that ...
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: But I would view these levels of burnout, of worry as worrisome.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: So if you have an extremely effective manager, for example, you're reporting around 65% higher job enjoyment and dramatically lower burnout.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: The impact that has on people's enjoyment, burnout and other aspects as well is incredible.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: We've linked in the article to a burnout test.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: Take the test, see where you're at on the burnout scale.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And so, if you want to improve burnout, if you want to elevate enjoyment, if you want to improve retention with these crazy job offers flying around from AI labs, you have to invest in your managers.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: I want to double down on that burnout survey that you mentioned.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: I've definitely gone through periods of burnout and wasn't aware that that was happening.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

entities.name: 认知衰退 (cognitive rot)
entities.how_described: 本集把它说成是过度依赖 AI 的代价：人们看到 AI 的产出就直接全盘接受，放弃独立思考与判断。受访者原话描述这种状态为「我的大脑正在腐烂」。

evidence.quote: And this is related to phenomenon I've heard about recently, is it cognitive rot?
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: We need to put a little bit more energy into it, into fighting this cognitive rot that we're feeling.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: 同薪挤压 (squeeze)
entities.how_described: 本集指出科技人最大的恐惧不是被 AI 取代，而是这种「以同样的薪水被期望做更多的事」的过度劳累挤压。AI 解锁的速度直接被转化为了对员工的期望。

evidence.quote: And then the second thing is, we talked earlier about being overworked, about this squeeze that AI has on us.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: Who's the person more than anyone else who manages that squeeze?
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: I also would love for people to watch out for this squeeze, being overworked for the same pay.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: The other is what you said, just watch the squeeze.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: And then otherwise, manage that squeeze.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: NPS
entities.how_described: 本集借用该指标（区间 -100 到 +100）来衡量科技人是否愿推荐亲友入行。结果显示目前没有任何一个科技岗位的 NPS 为正，连最幸福的创始人也不例外。

evidence.quote: I even built a website dedicated to fighting NPS, npsistheworst.com.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: Fun fact, by the way, I dislike NPS so much that I even brought to the domain npsisthebest.com, and I forward people from that to npsistheworst.com.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: That's how committed I am to fighting NPS.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: So let me show you the data around this, and let me preface this with a quick explanation on NPS because it's not so easy to understand.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: NPS is this question around whether you would recommend a product, or in this case your role to a friend or family member, right?
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: But then NPS as a whole, when it's calculated, goes from minus 100, which is absolutely terrible, to plus 100.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: It's not a promoter to use NPS speak, meaning I would promote my role to other people.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And then finally, when it comes to these NPS-like scores of whether people would recommend their role to a friend or to a colleague, researchers and designers are the least likely to recommend entering their role at this point.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: 技能萎缩 (skill atrophy)
entities.how_described: 本集提到由于有了 AI 这个捷径，人们本能地不再去刻意练习和深度思考，导致自身编码或写战略文档等敏锐度与能力下降。

evidence.quote: You had Simon Willison on this show talking about how people are worried about skill atrophy.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: 经理有效性 (manager effectiveness)
entities.how_described: 本集反复证明它是决定员工生死的最强杠杆：如果有极其有效的经理，工作享受度会高出约 65%，倦怠感大幅降低。但悲剧在于只有约 25% 的人认为自己的经理是高效的。

evidence.quote: And we found this very explicitly last year and this year again, manager effectiveness.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: 大扁平化 (great flattening)
entities.how_described: 本集提到当前科技圈「移除层级、保持组织扁平、让经理拥有更多直系下属」的趋势，并担忧在 AI 疯狂挤压员工的背景下，这会削弱经理保护员工免受过度压榨的能力。

evidence.quote: First of all, we are in the era of the great flattening.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: 冒名顶替综合征 (imposter phenomenon)
entities.how_described: 本集将其描述为「能干的人觉得自己的成功不属于自己」，并指出在 AI 时代的变体是「AI 内疚感」：早期职业生涯的年轻人觉得使用 AI 像是在作弊。

evidence.quote: This is related to the well-known and well-established imposter phenomenon, "I'm not good enough."
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: Cognition
entities.how_described: 本集提到这家开发 AI 软件工程师 Devin 的公司，并借用其 CEO 描述的「能力阶梯」比喻，来解释初级岗位的横档正在被 AI 快速抽走，导致没人愿意推荐新人入行。

evidence.quote: I like to use certain metaphors or examples from other areas in my life to explain these things, so I believe you spoke in the past to Scott Wu, the Co-Founder and CEO of Cognition.

evidence.quote: I loved your conversation with Jeff from Ramp, and he shared that his worst burnout happened when velocity was lowest.
